highlights.text: I wrote last Friday at 4pm: “The bull case Monday is that ES can do some back-testing of the zones we broke down yesterday and today.
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “ My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
Today, my lean was ES would squeeze it higher and I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
I call this “elevator down” and it sees ES lose many supports rapidly.
2) All “elevator down” sells in ES resolve with a violent short squeeze.
These short squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
A Failed Breakdown is when price sweeps a low, traps shorts, then recovers.
As a result we went elevator down into Friday’s 6240 low.
Last Friday at 230PM, ES put in a massive Failed Breakdown of a low at 6245.
On Tuesday ES went “elevator down” down to 6315 low of day.
Step 2: The Short Squeeze, caused by a Failed Breakdown.
You get the drill now, Failed Breakdown late day, get long again, short squeeze, then back to 6374 by the close Thursday.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a quality Failed Breakdown we had on Tuesday evening at 6:20PM below 6315 which triggered most of this weeks strength (this was provided in advance) and I’ll discuss the actionable trade plan for Monday.

post_title: Massive One Month Base For SPX. Will Next Week Bring The Big Breakout? August 11 Plan
